newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9. The ZONE 2026 launches (Nucleus, Workcloud BI/AI suite) were announced June 2 - pre-window and frontline software, not robotics - and the beat-and-raise quarter (April-end, sales $1.50B, adj EPS $4.75) predates the window. The +4% June 22 pop is sentiment, not a new fundamental event.

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent-AI-node (sensors/operational-data) lens: the Nucleus/Workcloud AI launches (Jun 2, just pre-window) modestly reinforce the data-capture-feeds-AI thesis, on top of a prior beat-and-raise. Constructive but not a fresh in-window catalyst; small +1.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Machine vision, RFID and Nucleus/Workcloud AI software monetizing operational data
verdicts.primaryRisk: Warehouse/retail capex recovery still uneven; large cap with cyclical exposure
verdicts.bull: Beat-and-raise quarter plus new AI software (Nucleus, Workcloud BI) deepen Zebra's role supplying clean operational data for AI.
verdicts.neutral: Endpoints/data-capture thesis intact but hinges on a warehouse-spend recovery that is only partial.
verdicts.bear: Hardware demand remains cyclical; software is early and won't quickly offset capex softness.

verdicts.rankRationale: Humanoid-stack lens: nothing since June 9 rebuilds the robotics case. The Skild sale stripped direct robotics upside and the recent ZONE news is frontline software, not robotics. Continue migrating the name down/off this page.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: None direct; residual Apera AI venture stake is not a robotics thesis
verdicts.primaryRisk: Skild divestiture removed direct robotics exposure; no humanoid-stack catalyst
verdicts.bull: Skild sale validated the asset's value; Zebra retains adjacent machine-vision/automation capability.
verdicts.neutral: Robotics is no longer a direct driver post-Skild; name belongs lower on a humanoid-stack page.
verdicts.bear: Skild divestiture removed direct robotics upside; recent launches are frontline software, not robotics.
